subject: Sports
title: ACL Tears: The Dreaded Injury
description: A short lesson to help you apply ACL Tears: The Dreaded Injury.

hook.text: A non-contact pivot, a pop, and a season is over. The ACL tear is the most feared injury in sport.

buildup.text: The anterior cruciate ligament stabilizes the knee. When it tears, the joint loses its central anchor.

discovery.text: ACL reconstruction uses a graft from another tendon. Recovery takes 9-12 months of intensive rehabilitation.

twist.text: Women athletes tear their ACLs 2-8 times more often than men—differences in anatomy and hormones play a role.

climax.text: Prevention programs focusing on landing mechanics and strength have reduced ACL injuries by up to 50%.

punchline.text: The most feared injury in sport is now 50% more preventable.

quiz.question: Why do women athletes experience more ACL tears than men?
options: Their ligaments are made of different tissue
